newsSinceJun6: No material AI-thesis news since June 6. Items in circulation are AGM housekeeping ahead of the June 26 shareholder meeting: dividend payout ratio raised from 30% to 40% with a Y130 year-end dividend and a 6-for-1 share split, a new restricted-stock director comp plan, and an auditor switch from PwC Japan to Deloitte. The Y300bn (~$2B) plan to triple SWR/WTC optical fiber capacity in Japan and the US dates from March 2026 and is already embedded in the thesis. Stock +2.23% today (drift), +46% YTD but ~43% below its 7,933 52-week high.

sources.label: TipRanks - Fujikura lifts dividend payout ratio and year-end dividend after strong FY2026 results
TipRanks - Fujikura to replace long-standing auditor PwC Japan with Deloitte
w.media - Fujikura to triple fiber capacity with Y300bn AI push (March 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a latent/hidden-node list Fujikura is already scored as a fully discovered control name at rank 90; nothing since June 6 changes that, and yesterday's down move already reconciled the adjusted rank. A +2.2% drift day on a $49B cap with only AGM housekeeping news warrants a small negative drift, not another rank move.
verdicts.bull: Tripling SWR/WTC fiber capacity into US AI data center demand with a new Delaware subsidiary keeps Fujikura structurally levered to cluster cabling, and the 43% pullback from the 7,933 high plus a 40% payout ratio restores some asymmetry.
verdicts.neutral: Strong Buy consensus (9 of 12 buys, ~5,837 average target) and +46% YTD confirm the crowd has fully found this name; it now trades on AI capex beta rather than discovery, consistent with its control-name designation.
verdicts.bear: At $49B market cap with sell-side saturation, any AI optical capex digestion or fiber pricing normalization hits a stock that has already rerated; the March capacity plan is also explicitly conditioned on market conditions.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Execution updates on the Y300bn Japan/US fiber capacity tripling and new Sakura plant, plus FY guidance around the June 26 AGM.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding: a discovered, consensus-Strong-Buy $49B name with +46% YTD has little residual hidden-node alpha if AI data center cabling demand decelerates.
